article_meta.title: Former President Donald Trump speaks at a campaign rally on April 27, 2023, in Manchester, New Hampshire

reasoning: The model avoids flagging quoted text as author bias (good attribution rule compliance). However, both flagged items are somewhat questionable. The first flags the story choice/placement noting the 'Opinion' label, which is a meta-observation rather than identifying specific biased text in the author's prose. The second flags 'Negativity Bias' based on structural framing/omission, which is defensible but the 'biasedText' field describes a pattern rather than quoting actual text. Meanwhile, the model misses several clear instances of author-level bias in the prose: 'But Trump has given listeners no reason to believe him on this' (Opinion/Spin), 'people should treat his story with great skepticism' (Opinion Statements Presented as Fact), 'dramatic story' (Subjective Qualifying Adjective/Word Choice), 'Trump added more color to the story' (Word Choice suggesting embellishment), 'late Cuban despot Fidel Castro' (Word Choice - 'despot'), 'Breitbart's vague and unverified claim' (Subjective Qualifying Adjectives). These are clear, specific instances of author-level bias that were missed.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: The model correctly avoided flagging any quoted text from Trump, Cheung, or other sources. All flagged items are about the author's structural/editorial choices.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Both flagged items are debatable. The Bias by Story Choice item references a meta-observation about the article being labeled 'Opinion' rather than specific biased text. The Negativity Bias item describes a structural pattern rather than quoting specific text. Neither is clearly wrong but neither points to specific author prose either.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Multiple clear author-level bias instances were missed: 'dramatic story' (Subjective Qualifying Adjective), 'But Trump has given listeners no reason to believe him on this' (Opinion/Spin), 'people should treat his story with great skepticism' (Opinion Presented as Fact), 'late Cuban despot Fidel Castro' (Word Choice), 'Breitbart's vague and unverified claim' (Subjective Qualifying Adjectives), 'Trump added more color to the story' (Word Choice implying embellishment). These are significant misses.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: The two types chosen (Bias by Story Choice and Placement, Negativity Bias) are defensible for the structural observations made, though somewhat debatable in application. The labels aren't clearly wrong but aren't precisely right either given the vague biasedText fields.
